original_abstract: This book is a collection of essays by prominent historians from Austria, Germany, Ukraine, Poland and Russia, who undertook a thorough and detailed study of one as yet inadequately researched aspect of the First World War--the occupation of Ukraine by the Central Powers in 1918. The book provides a new and fresh perspective on the historical context of Ukraine’s struggle for independence following the First World War.
humanized_abstract: Contributions by historians from Austria, Germany, Ukraine, Poland and Russia. All experts have carried out a thorough and highly specialized examination of one of the least researched chapters of the First World War: the Central Powers’ occupation of Ukraine in 1918. This book presents a whole new approach to understanding the history of Ukraine’s struggle for independence in the aftermath of the First World War.

This book presents a whole new approach to understanding the history of Ukraine’s struggle for independence in the aftermath of the First World War.
